source_documents: ICH M4 - Organisation of the Common Technical Document (M4_CTD_Organization.pdf)
metadata.notes: Module 1 is region-specific and not part of the ICH harmonised CTD. Its structure varies by regulatory authority.

general.orientation: Portrait preferred; landscape for wide tables
general.pagination: Each Module 2-5 section starts on a new page
general.table_of_contents: Required for each module, generated per module

citations.numbering: Sequential as they appear in text

tables_and_figures.placement: Embedded in text where they aid readability; lengthy tables in appendix

ectd_v4_keywords.substance: Identifies the drug substance (INN or code name)
ectd_v4_keywords.manufacturer: Identifies the manufacturer of drug substance or drug product
ectd_v4_keywords.excipient: Optional; identifies excipient for 3.2.P.4 and 3.2.A.3 granularity
ectd_v4_keywords.descriptor: Optional; used for stability data (3.2.S.7.3, 3.2.P.8.3) differentiation
ectd_v4_keywords.container: Optional; generates separate 3.2.P.7 sections per container closure system
ectd_v4_keywords.facility: Optional; generates separate 2.3.A.1 / 3.2.A.1 sections per facility
ectd_v4_keywords.component: Optional; generates separate 2.3.A.2 / 3.2.A.2 sections per drug substance component

module_1.title: Regional Administrative Information
module_1.description: Not part of the harmonised CTD. Content and format are defined by each regional regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA).

1.1.title: Comprehensive Table of Contents
1.1.description: Complete TOC for all modules of the application

module_1.notes: Structure varies by region. US FDA, EU EMA, Japan PMDA each define their own Module 1 content.

module_2.title: Common Technical Document Summaries
module_2.description: High-level summaries and overviews of Modules 3, 4, and 5 data. This is the core review document for regulatory assessors.

2.1.title: CTD Table of Contents
2.1.description: Complete table of contents for Modules 2-5
data_elements: Full section listing with page references for Modules 2-5

2.2.title: CTD Introduction
2.2.description: Brief introduction including pharmacological class, mode of action, proposed clinical use. General introductory information about the drug.

2.3.title: Quality Overall Summary (QOS)
2.3.description: Summary of Module 3 quality data. Should present the applicant's conclusions with cross-references to detailed data in Module 3.

2.3.S.1.title: General Information
data_elements: Structure (structural formula, molecular formula, molecular weight)

2.3.S.2.title: Manufacture
data_elements: Description of manufacturing process and process controls
Controls of critical steps and intermediates

2.3.S.3.title: Characterisation
data_elements: Elucidation of structure and other characteristics

2.3.S.7.title: Stability
data_elements: Post-approval stability protocol and stability commitment

2.3.P.1.title: Description and Composition of the Drug Product

2.3.P.2.title: Pharmaceutical Development
data_elements: Components of the drug product (drug substance, excipients, compatibility)

2.3.P.3.title: Manufacture
data_elements: Description of manufacturing process and process controls
Controls of critical steps and intermediates

2.3.P.4.title: Control of Excipients
data_elements: Excipients of human or animal origin

2.3.P.6.title: Reference Standards or Materials
data_elements: Reference standards used for drug product testing

2.3.P.8.title: Stability
data_elements: Post-approval stability protocol and stability commitment

2.3.A.1.title: Facilities and Equipment
data_elements: Facility information (optional keyword-based granularity in eCTD v4)

cross_references.description: Each QOS Drug Substance subsection must reference the corresponding Module 3 body of data section
Each QOS Drug Product subsection must reference the corresponding Module 3 body of data section
Each QOS Appendix subsection must reference the corresponding Module 3 appendix
QOS introduction should state where Module 3 control strategy summaries are located

2.4.title: Nonclinical Overview
2.4.description: Integrated assessment of nonclinical evaluation of the drug. Interpretive narrative, not a summary of study results.
data_elements: Toxicology overview (single-dose, repeat-dose, genotoxicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity)

cross_references.description: Overview should reference written/tabulated summaries for detailed data
Must reference detailed study reports
When discussing impurity qualification, must cross-reference quality documentation

2.5.title: Clinical Overview
2.5.description: Critical analysis of the clinical data. Provides a clinical perspective on the benefits and risks of the product.

2.5.2.title: Overview of Biopharmaceutics
data_elements: Comparative BA of clinical and marketed formulations

2.5.5.title: Overview of Safety
data_elements: Use in special populations (pregnancy, paediatric, geriatric, renal/hepatic impairment)

2.5.6.1.title: Context for Assessment of Benefits and Risks

2.5.6.2.title: Summary of Benefits
data_elements: Key efficacy results supporting benefit claim

2.5.6.4.title: Risk Management
data_elements: Risk mitigation strategies, REMS if applicable

2.5.7.title: Literature References
data_elements: All references cited in the Clinical Overview

cross_references.description: Overview must reference Clinical Summary for detailed factual data
Must reference individual study reports for source data
Safety overview should reference nonclinical safety findings where relevant
Safety overview may reference quality data (impurities, formulation) where relevant

2.6.title: Nonclinical Written and Tabulated Summaries
2.6.description: Detailed factual summaries with tabulated data for all nonclinical studies. Includes both written narrative summaries and standardised tabulated summaries.

2.6.1.title: Introduction
data_elements: Brief description of the drug substance (name, pharmacological class, structural formula)

2.6.2.1.title: Brief Summary
data_elements: Concise overall summary of pharmacology findings

2.6.2.2.title: Primary Pharmacodynamics
data_elements: In vitro and in vivo studies on target pharmacology, mechanism of action

2.6.2.3.title: Secondary Pharmacodynamics
data_elements: Activity at other than the primary target

2.6.3.title: Pharmacology Tabulated Summary
data_elements: Tabulated summary template with columns: Type of Study, Test System, Method of Administration, Testing Facility, Study Number, Location (Vol/Section)

2.6.4.4.title: Distribution
data_elements: Tissue distribution, protein binding, placental transfer, blood-brain barrier

2.6.4.5.title: Metabolism
data_elements: In vivo and in vitro metabolism, metabolic pathways, enzyme identification

2.6.4.6.title: Excretion
data_elements: Routes and rate of excretion, mass balance

2.6.4.9.title: Discussion and Conclusions
data_elements: Interpretation of PK data, species comparison, human prediction

2.6.5.title: Pharmacokinetics Tabulated Summary
data_elements: Standardised tabulated templates per study type

2.6.5.2.title: Analytical Methods and Validation Reports (tabulated summary optional)

2.6.5.3.title: Pharmacokinetics: Absorption after a Single Dose
data_elements: Species, Gender, Feeding condition, Vehicle, Route, Dose, Sample type, Analyte, Assay, PK parameters

2.6.5.5.title: Pharmacokinetics: Organ Distribution
data_elements: Species, Dose, Radionuclide, Specific Activity, Sampling time, Tissue concentrations, t1/2

2.6.5.6.title: Pharmacokinetics: Plasma Protein Binding
data_elements: Study system, Target entity, Method, Species, Conc. tested, % Bound

2.6.5.7.title: Pharmacokinetics: Study in Pregnant or Nursing Animals
data_elements: Placental transfer data, Excretion into milk data

2.6.5.9.title: Pharmacokinetics: Metabolism In Vivo
data_elements: Species, Sample (Plasma/Urine/Bile/Feces), % of Compound (Parent, M1, M2...)

2.6.6.2.title: Single-Dose Toxicity
data_elements: LD50/approximate lethal dose, target organs, reversibility

2.6.6.3.title: Repeat-Dose Toxicity
data_elements: NOAEL, target organs, dose-response, reversibility, exposure margins

2.6.6.4.title: Genotoxicity
data_elements: In vitro and in vivo genotoxicity study results

2.6.6.5.title: Carcinogenicity
data_elements: Long-term carcinogenicity studies, statistical analyses, mechanism of tumorigenesis

2.6.6.7.title: Local Tolerance
data_elements: Local tolerance studies at site of administration

2.6.7.11.title: Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity: Non-Pivotal Studies

2.6.7.12.title: Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity - Fertility and Early Embryonic Development to Implantation (Pivotal)

2.6.7.13.title: Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity - Effects on Embryo-Fetal Development (Pivotal)

2.6.7.14.title: Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity - Effects on Pre- and Postnatal Development, Including Maternal Function (Pivotal)

cross_references.description: Written summaries must reference individual study reports in Module 4
When discussing impurity qualification in toxicology, must cross-reference quality documentation
Each tabulated summary row must include Location in CTD (Vol/Section) pointing to the full report

2.7.title: Clinical Summary
2.7.description: Detailed factual summaries of all clinical data. Bridge between the Clinical Overview (2.5) and the individual study reports (Module 5).

2.7.1.title: Summary of Biopharmaceutic Studies and Associated Analytical Methods

2.7.1.1.title: Background and Overview
data_elements: Analytical methods for measuring drug concentrations in biological fluids

2.7.1.2.title: Summary of Results of Individual Studies

2.7.1.3.title: Comparison and Analyses of Results Across Studies

2.7.1.4.title: Appendix
data_elements: Tabular formats for BA and dissolution studies (Tables 2.7.1.1, 2.7.1.2)

2.7.2.1.title: Background and Overview
data_elements: Clinical PK and PD studies overview
Study design and data analysis approach

2.7.2.2.title: Summary of Results of Individual Studies

2.7.2.3.title: Comparison and Analyses of Results Across Studies
data_elements: In vitro drug metabolism and drug-drug interaction results
Human PK parameters and sources of variability

2.7.2.5.title: Appendix
data_elements: Table 2.7.2.1 (tabular format for drug-drug interaction studies)
Tables for metabolism, population PK studies

2.7.3.title: Summary of Clinical Efficacy
2.7.3.description: Separate section per indication. When multiple indications, label as 2.7.3 pneumonia, 2.7.3 URI, etc.

2.7.3.1.title: Background and Overview of Clinical Efficacy
data_elements: Critical design features (randomisation, blinding, endpoints, duration)

2.7.3.2.title: Summary of Results of Individual Studies
data_elements: Tabular listing of all efficacy studies (see 2.7.3.6 Appendix)
Brief narrative descriptions of important studies (ICH E3 synopsis format)

2.7.3.3.title: Comparison and Analyses of Results Across Studies

2.7.3.3.1.title: Study Populations
data_elements: Disease characteristics, severity, duration, prior treatment

2.7.3.3.2.title: Comparison of Efficacy Results of All Studies
data_elements: All efficacy results compared including negative/inconclusive studies

2.7.3.3.3.title: Comparison of Results in Sub-populations
data_elements: Efficacy in age, sex, race subgroups

2.7.3.4.title: Analysis of Clinical Information Relevant to Dosing Recommendations
data_elements: Dose modifications for subgroups (paediatric, geriatric, renal/hepatic)

2.7.3.5.title: Persistence of Efficacy and/or Tolerance Effects
data_elements: Evidence of tolerance (loss of therapeutic effect over time)

2.7.3.6.title: Appendix
data_elements: Table 2.7.3.1 (Description of Clinical Efficacy and Safety Studies)

2.7.4.title: Summary of Clinical Safety
2.7.4.description: Integrated summary of safety data from all clinical studies.

2.7.4.1.1.title: Overall Safety Evaluation Plan and Narratives of Safety Studies
data_elements: Tabular listing of all safety studies (see 2.7.4.7 Appendix)

2.7.4.1.2.title: Overall Extent of Exposure
data_elements: Numbers of subjects by study type, dose, route, duration

2.7.4.1.3.title: Demographic and Other Characteristics of Study Population
data_elements: Special characteristics (severity, hospitalisation, renal function, concomitant illness/medications, geography)

2.7.4.2.1.1.title: Common Adverse Events
data_elements: Drug-related factors (dosage, regimen, duration, demographics, concomitant medications, drug concentration)

2.7.4.2.1.3.title: Other Serious Adverse Events
data_elements: Serious adverse events per ICH E2A definition
Major laboratory abnormalities, vital sign abnormalities

2.7.4.2.1.4.title: Other Significant Adverse Events
data_elements: Events leading to dose reduction or discontinuation

2.7.4.2.1.5.title: Analysis of Adverse Events by Organ System or Syndrome
data_elements: AEs organised by organ system (subsections 2.7.4.2.1.5.1, 2.7.4.2.1.5.2, etc.)

2.7.4.2.2.title: Narratives
data_elements: Individual narratives of deaths, SAEs, significant AEs
Cross-reference to ICH E3 individual study reports

2.7.4.3.title: Clinical Laboratory Evaluations
data_elements: Central tendency (mean, median) of lab values

2.7.4.4.title: Vital Signs, Physical Findings, and Other Observations Related to Safety
data_elements: Vital signs (HR, BP, temperature, respiratory rate)

2.7.4.5.title: Safety in Special Groups and Situations

2.7.4.5.1.title: Intrinsic Factors
data_elements: Age, sex, height, weight, lean body mass

2.7.4.5.3.title: Drug Interactions
data_elements: Drug-drug and drug-food interaction safety data
Cross-reference to 2.7.2 for PK interaction data

2.7.4.5.6.title: Drug Abuse
data_elements: Dependence potential in animals and humans

2.7.4.5.8.title: Effects on Ability to Drive or Operate Machinery or Impairment of Mental Ability

2.7.4.6.title: Post-marketing Data
data_elements: Post-marketing exposure estimates (by indication, dosage, route, duration, geography)

2.7.4.7.title: Appendix
data_elements: Table 2.7.4.1 (Drug Exposure by Mean Daily Dose and Duration)
Table 2.7.4.2 (Demographic Profile of Patients in Controlled Trials)
Table 2.7.4.3 (Incidence of Adverse Events in Pooled Placebo and Active Controlled Trials)
Table 2.7.4.4 (Incidence of Adverse Events in the Largest Trials)
Table 2.7.4.5 (Patient Withdrawals by Study: Controlled Trials)

2.7.5.title: Literature References
data_elements: All references cited in the Clinical Summary
Indication of which references are available in Module 5, Section 5.4

2.7.6.title: Synopses of Individual Studies
data_elements: Individual study synopses (ICH E3 format, 3-10 pages each)
Same sequence as study reports in Module 5

cross_references.description: All clinical summary subsections must reference individual study reports in Module 5
Efficacy comparison must cross-reference PK/PD evidence from Section 2.7.2
Drug abuse section must cross-reference nonclinical dependence studies
Safety drug interaction section must cross-reference PK interaction data in 2.7.2
Synopses must be organised in same sequence as Module 5 reports

module_3.title: Quality
module_3.description: Body of data for drug substance and drug product quality (CMC). Contains the detailed data that supports the Quality Overall Summary (2.3).

3.1.title: Module 3 Table of Contents
data_elements: Table of contents for all Module 3 sections

3.2.S.title: Drug Substance
3.2.S.note: Repeat for each drug substance. In eCTD v4, the 'substance' keyword differentiates multiple drug substances.

3.2.S.2.1.title: Manufacturer(s)
data_elements: Name, address, responsibility of each manufacturer, including contractors

3.2.S.2.2.title: Description of Manufacturing Process and Process Controls

3.2.S.2.4.title: Controls of Critical Steps and Intermediates

3.2.S.3.1.title: Elucidation of Structure and other Characteristics

3.2.S.4.4.title: Batch Analyses
data_elements: Batch results (description, batch size, date, site)

3.2.S.4.5.title: Justification of Specification
data_elements: Scientific rationale for each test and acceptance criterion

3.2.S.5.title: Reference Standards or Materials
data_elements: Source, characterisation, purity of reference standards

3.2.S.7.2.title: Post-Approval Stability Protocol and Stability Commitment

3.2.P.title: Drug Product
3.2.P.note: Repeat for each drug product/dosage form. In eCTD v4, 'product' and 'dosage_form' keywords differentiate.

3.2.P.1.title: Description and Composition of the Drug Product
data_elements: Composition table (all components, function, quality standard per ICH M4Q)

3.2.P.2.2.3.title: Physicochemical and Biological Properties
data_elements: Relevant product properties (pH, dissolution, particle size, etc.)

3.2.P.2.6.title: Compatibility
data_elements: Compatibility with reconstitution diluents, devices, etc.

3.2.P.3.2.title: Batch Formula
data_elements: Batch formula for representative batch size

3.2.P.3.3.title: Description of Manufacturing Process and Process Controls

3.2.P.3.4.title: Controls of Critical Steps and Intermediates

3.2.P.4.title: Control of Excipients
3.2.P.4.note: Granularity options: single 3.2.P.4 section, or multiple using 'excipient' keyword in eCTD v4.

3.2.P.4.4.title: Justification of Specifications
data_elements: Rationale for non-compendial or additional tests

3.2.P.4.5.title: Excipients of Human or Animal Origin

3.2.P.4.6.title: Novel Excipients
data_elements: Full supporting data package for novel excipients

3.2.P.6.title: Reference Standards or Materials
data_elements: Reference standards for drug product testing

3.2.P.7.title: Container Closure System
3.2.P.7.note: Optional 'container' keyword in eCTD v4 for multiple container closure systems.
data_elements: Suitability, protection, compatibility, safety, performance studies

3.2.P.8.2.title: Post-Approval Stability Protocol and Stability Commitment

3.2.P.8.3.title: Stability Data
data_elements: Long-term, accelerated, and stress stability data

3.2.A.1.title: Facilities and Equipment
3.2.A.1.note: Optional 'facility' keyword in eCTD v4 for site-level granularity.

3.2.A.2.title: Adventitious Agents Safety Evaluation
3.2.A.2.note: Optional 'component' keyword in eCTD v4 for multi-substance products.
data_elements: Viral safety evaluation (for biologics: virus clearance, testing)

3.2.A.3.title: Excipients
data_elements: Detailed data for novel excipients or non-compendial excipients

3.2.R.title: Regional Information
data_elements: Region-specific quality information as required by local authority

cross_references.description: Module 3 drug substance data supports QOS in Module 2
Module 3 drug product data supports QOS in Module 2
Impurity characterisation data must be connected to nonclinical qualification studies when required by ICH Q3A/Q3B

ich_guidelines_referenced: Q3A(R2) - Impurities in New Drug Substances
Q3B(R2) - Impurities in New Drug Products
Q11 - Development and Manufacture of Drug Substances

module_4.title: Nonclinical Study Reports
module_4.description: Full study reports for all nonclinical (animal/in-vitro) studies. Organised by discipline (pharmacology, pharmacokinetics, toxicology).

4.1.title: Module 4 Table of Contents
data_elements: Table of contents for all Module 4 sections

4.2.1.1.title: Primary Pharmacodynamics
data_elements: Full study reports of primary pharmacology studies

4.2.1.2.title: Secondary Pharmacodynamics
data_elements: Full study reports of secondary pharmacology studies

4.2.2.3.title: Distribution
data_elements: Tissue distribution, protein binding, blood cell partitioning studies

4.2.2.4.title: Metabolism
data_elements: In vivo and in vitro metabolism study reports

4.2.3.1.title: Single-Dose Toxicity
data_elements: Single-dose toxicity study reports, ordered by species then route

4.2.3.2.title: Repeat-Dose Toxicity
data_elements: Repeat-dose toxicity study reports, ordered by species then duration

4.2.3.3.1.title: In Vitro
data_elements: Ames test, chromosome aberration, gene mutation studies

4.2.3.3.2.title: In Vivo
data_elements: Micronucleus, comet assay, other in vivo genotoxicity studies

4.2.3.5.2.title: Embryo-Fetal Development
data_elements: Segment II study reports, ordered by species

4.2.3.5.3.title: Prenatal and Postnatal Development, Including Maternal Function

4.2.3.5.4.title: Studies in which the Offspring (Juvenile Animals) Are Dosed and/or Further Evaluated

4.2.3.7.6.title: Impurities
data_elements: Impurity qualification studies (per ICH Q3A/Q3B thresholds)

4.2.3.7.7.title: Other
data_elements: Phototoxicity, combination toxicity, or other studies

cross_references.description: Study reports provide source data for the Nonclinical Overview
Study reports provide source data for the Written and Tabulated Summaries
Impurity qualification studies must be cross-referenced with quality impurity characterisation

study_ordering_rules.within_each_section: Order by species (standard ordering), then by route (intended route first, then standard route ordering), then by duration (shortest to longest)

ich_guidelines_referenced: S4 - Duration of Chronic Toxicity Studies
S7B - Nonclinical Evaluation of QT Prolongation
S9 - Nonclinical Evaluation for Anticancer Pharmaceuticals

module_5.title: Clinical Study Reports
module_5.description: Full clinical study reports (ICH E3 format) and related information. Contains all clinical data that supports the Clinical Overview (2.5) and Clinical Summary (2.7).

5.1.title: Module 5 Table of Contents
data_elements: Table of contents for all Module 5 sections, including Listing of Clinical Studies table

5.2.title: Tabular Listing of All Clinical Studies
data_elements: Type of report (full, abbreviated, synopsis only)

5.3.1.1.title: Bioavailability (BA) Study Reports
data_elements: Full BA study reports per ICH E3

5.3.1.2.title: Comparative BA and Bioequivalence (BE) Study Reports

5.3.1.3.title: In Vitro - In Vivo Correlation Study Reports

5.3.1.4.title: Reports of Bioanalytical and Analytical Methods for Human Studies
data_elements: Bioanalytical method validation reports for clinical samples

5.3.2.title: Reports of Studies Pertinent to Pharmacokinetics Using Human Biomaterials

5.3.2.2.title: Reports of Hepatic Metabolism and Drug Interaction Studies
data_elements: In vitro metabolism, CYP identification, inhibition/induction studies

5.3.2.3.title: Reports of Studies Using Other Human Biomaterials
data_elements: Other in vitro human biomaterial studies (transporter, permeability, etc.)

5.3.3.1.title: Healthy Subject PK and Initial Tolerability Study Reports
data_elements: Phase I PK studies in healthy volunteers

5.3.3.2.title: Patient PK and Initial Tolerability Study Reports
data_elements: PK studies in target patient population

5.3.3.3.title: Intrinsic Factor PK Study Reports
data_elements: Renal impairment, hepatic impairment, age, sex, race, genetic polymorphism PK studies

5.3.3.4.title: Extrinsic Factor PK Study Reports
data_elements: Drug-drug interaction, food effect, other extrinsic factor PK studies

5.3.4.1.title: Healthy Subject PD and PK/PD Study Reports
data_elements: PD and PK/PD studies in healthy volunteers

5.3.4.2.title: Patient PD and PK/PD Study Reports
data_elements: PD and PK/PD studies in patients

5.3.5.title: Reports of Efficacy and Safety Studies

5.3.5.1.title: Study Reports of Controlled Clinical Studies Pertinent to the Claimed Indication
data_elements: Pivotal and supportive controlled efficacy study reports (ICH E3 format)

5.3.5.2.title: Study Reports of Uncontrolled Clinical Studies

5.3.5.3.title: Reports of Analyses of Data from More than One Study

5.3.5.4.title: Other Study Reports
data_elements: Other clinical studies (e.g., withdrawal studies, dose-response, concentration-response)

5.3.7.title: Case Report Forms and Individual Patient Listings
data_elements: CRFs for deaths, serious AEs, AEs leading to withdrawal
Individual patient data listings as needed
5.3.7.note: When to include is defined by ICH E3 and regional requirements

5.4.title: Literature References
data_elements: Copies of all important references cited in Modules 2.5, 2.7, and Module 5

cross_references.description: Study reports provide source data for the Clinical Overview
Study reports provide source data for the Clinical Summary. 2.7.6 synopses must match Module 5 sequence.
Integrated analyses referenced in the clinical summaries should be placed here
Full copies of cited references should be available in 5.4; reference lists in 2.5 and 2.7 should indicate which are in 5.4

study_report_format.standard: ICH E3 - Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports

ich_guidelines_referenced: E1 - Population Exposure for Chronic Conditions
E3 - Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports
E8(R1) - General Considerations for Clinical Studies

global_cross_reference_map.description: Summary of all mandatory cross-reference relationships between modules

relationships.description: QOS summarises Module 3; each 2.3.X section references corresponding 3.2.X section
Overview references detailed written and tabulated summaries
Written and tabulated summaries reference individual study reports with Location in CTD
Clinical summaries reference individual study reports
Clinical safety overview may reference nonclinical findings
Clinical safety overview may reference quality/impurity data
Impurity qualification in nonclinical must cross-reference quality impurity profiles
Clinical drug abuse section must reference nonclinical dependence data
Safety interaction data references PK interaction studies
Efficacy comparison must cross-reference PK/PD evidence
Synopses must match the sequence of study reports in Module 5
Full copies of cited literature in 5.4; reference lists indicate availability
